package_description: Small combustion-side heat-recovery and insulation package for incumbent high-temperature fossil heat systems.
rollout_limit_notes: Keep rollout deliberately narrow because high-temperature recovery opportunities are site-specific and broader savings belong in future subsector-specific modeling rather than a large family-level wedge.
evidence_summary: Only a small combustion-side recovery package is defensible at the current high-temperature family boundary; larger savings would blur into route change or process redesign.
derivation_method: Apply a modest proportional multiplier across direct thermal fuels and keep the cap low so the package remains a small wedge relative to high-temperature route-switch uncertainty.
review_notes: Fossil incumbent only. Shares a family-local non-stacking group with the high-temperature controls package.

package_description: Operational combustion tuning, turn-down, temperature-control, and maintenance package on incumbent high-temperature fossil heat systems.
rollout_limit_notes: Operational tuning is easier to scale than heavy heat-recovery retrofits, but keep the wedge small because the family is too stylised to justify a large generic high-temperature controls effect.
evidence_summary: High-temperature controls are a valid but small operational wedge on incumbent fossil systems, with most larger efficiency-looking gains deferred to process redesign or route change.
derivation_method: Use a deliberately small proportional multiplier and low service-cost adder so the package remains below the scale of the pure heat-recovery retrofit and far below route-change effects.
review_notes: Shares the family-local non-stacking group with combustion heat recovery.
